title: Amellus novi-belgii
intro:
There is no information available on the plant Amellus novi-belgii. However, Amellus albus is a synonym for Helianthus novi-belgii, which is a plant in the daisy family. 

Helianthus novi-belgii is a flowering plant, commonly known as the New York daisy or swamp daisy. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.

The bioactive constituents of Helianthus novi-belgii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds may have antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects.

The most common herbal preparations made with Helianthus novi-belgii include teas, infusions, and tinctures. However, it is essential to note that the effectiveness and safety of these preparations have not been extensively scientifically studied.
taxonomy: Amellus novi-belgii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to North America, specifically the northeastern United States and southeastern Canada. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is commonly known as a daisy-like plant. The classification of Amellus novi-belgii is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Asterales. This classification allows for a comprehensive understanding of the plant's evolutionary relationships and position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Amellus novi-belgii, commonly known as the New York ironweed, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have antiseptic properties, making them useful for treating wounds and skin infections. It has also been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Furthermore, its roots are believed to have a diuretic effect, which can help to relieve urinary tract issues. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe sore throats and coughs.
